series_description:
Including any supporting documentation
created and/or used to justify and support
legislative appropriations by an agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of Legislative
Appropriation Requests must be submitted to the
Texas State Publications Depository Program per 13
TAC 3.3(a)(3)(A). Working files and related
documentation used in creating the final plan are not
subject to archival review and may be disposed of at
the expiration of the retention period.

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by an agency from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes the subject of
litigation, it must be included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item number 1.1.048.

series_description:
Requests from a party to an informal
hearing for a continuance and responses
granting or denying the request.
comments:
A copy of the response is sent to the Litigation
Department and may, but is not required to, be
included as part of the Litigation file.

series_description:
Calendars, appointment books or
programs and scheduling, or itinerary
records maintained by staff during
business hours that document
appointments, itineraries, and other
activities of agency officials or employees.
retention_statement: Calendar Year End plus 1 year
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only the calendars, appointment,
and itinerary records of elected officials, agency heads,
and board or commission members require archival
review. Contact the State Archives when these records
have met their retention periods.
CAUTION: A record of this type purchased with
personal funds but used by a state official or employee
to document his or her work activities may be a state
record and subject to this retention period. See Open
Records Decision 635 issued in December 1995 by the
Attorney General.

series_description:
News, press releases, marketing files and
collateral, or any public relations files
maintained or issued by an agency.
Includes print, electronic, audio, and
audiovisual records.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 2 years
comments:
Agency note: Agency will submit records for archival
review when the retention period is met, except for
Press Releases which have been evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services Division of the Texas
State Library and Archives Commission and which are
now assigned archival code (A) on January 8, 2025.
Press Releases therefore must be transferred to the
State Archives for permanent retention at the end of
the retention period.

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are furnished to the public
under the Public Information Act (Chapter
552, Government Code). Also includes
withdrawn requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Includes all correspondence and
documentation relating to requests for
records that are:
(1) exempt under the Public Information
Act (Chapter 552, Government Code),
including records provided to the
legislature under section 552.008
OR
(2) provided to a legislator and/or
legislative committee as authorized by the
Medical Practice Act (§154.055,
Occupations Code).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: former series PI.007 was merged into PI.003.

series_description:
Charts or diagrams that show the structure
of an agency and the relationships and
relative ranks of its departments and job
positions.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Agency note: Archival requirement fulfilled through
submission of Legislative Appropriations Request (See
1.1.004).
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only charts showing the overall
arrangement and administrative structure of the state
agency need to be transferred to the Archives and
Information Services Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission. Organizational charts showing
division/department level detail are not considered
archival.

series_description:
Copies of all open meetings notices
including any other documents required by
law to be submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
EX = submits open meetings notices, including PHP
open meeting notices.
Note: Due to electronic submissions, original
documents are maintained in their records series and
this series contains only the email confirmation with
the link to the submission.
Formerly AIN. EX/GC.001

series_description:
Copies of all proposed, withdrawn,
emergency, and adopted rules;
stakeholder meetings notices; or any other
documents required by law to be
submitted to the Texas Register.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
GC = rule submissions, including PHP rules
submissions.
Note: Due to electronic submissions, original
documents are maintained in their records series.
Formerly AIN. EX/GC.001

series_description:
Contains all related records with the same
function and retention period.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Records management officers should make
certain records are not part of another records series
listed in this schedule or, for records series unique to
an agency, are not part of a records series that
documents the fulfillment of the statutory obligations
of the agency or the documentation of its functions.

series_description:
Notes or text of speeches, papers,
presentations, or reports delivered in
conjunction with agency work.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
Note 1: Agency will submit records for archival review
when the retention period is met. Includes statements,
speeches and/or written testimony presented at
Agency board meetings, before the legislature or
legislative committee hearings.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Only speeches, papers, and
presentations given by or on behalf of executive staff,
board or commission members, division directors and
program heads require archival review.

series_description:
Records created by or on behalf of an
agency in anticipation of or in the
adjudication of a lawsuit.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Note 1: Agency will submit records for archival review
when the retention period is met.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Cases that set legal precedent or
exhibit historical value will be evaluated by the
Archives and Information Services Division of the Texas
State Library and Archives Commission for archival
preservation. See page ix for more information.

series_description:
Operational strategic plans, which include
descriptions of information resources
programs, prepared in accordance with
Government Code, 2054.095 and
2056.002. Includes working files and
related documentation used in creating
the final plan.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 6 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: The final version of Strategic Plans
must be submitted to the Texas State Publications
Depository Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(2)(C). Working
files and related documentation used in creating the
final plan are not subject to archival review and may
be disposed of at the expiration of the retention
period.

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes or Audiovisual
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: SEE caution comment at item number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Notes taken during open meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils from which written minutes are
prepared.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 90 days
comments: SEE caution comment at item number 1.1.058.

series_title:
TMB Biennial Report on
Physician Licensure (non-
fiscal)
series_description:
Report on the state of the Board's licensing
process, produced no later than August 1
of each even-numbered year, and sent to
the governor, the Legislative Budget
Board, and the relevant committees of the
senate
and the house of representatives.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
This report is required by HB1973 (80th Legislature).
This report was evaluated by the Archives and
Information Services Division of the Texas State Library
and Archives Commission and was assigned archival
code (A) on January 8, 2025. The reports must be
transferred to the State Archives for permanent
retention at the end of the retention period.
ARCHIVES NOTE: Reports are archival when they deal
with significant aspects of the agency's programs. For
agencies that have had an archival appraisal, separate
this records series by each type of archival coding, A,
R, or E. See page ix of the State RRS for additional
guidelines.

series_description:
Manuals, guidelines, administrative rules,
or similar records distributed internally for
the use of employees or externally to the
public or those individuals or entities
regulated by an agency that sets out the
rules, policies, and procedures that govern
an agency's programs, services, or
projects. Includes working files and related
documentation used in creating rules,
policies, and procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Vital Record
Access to adopted final rules is provided in the Texas
Register and Texas Administrative Code.
TSLAC previously determined the archival code of E for
TMB Board Rules (2019).
ARCHIVES NOTE: Working files and related
documentation (including rule reviews) used in
creating the final rules are not subject to archival
requirement and may be disposed of at the expiration
of the retention period.

series_description:
Reports made to the Office of Attorney
General (OAG) on an agency's Public
Information Act activities, per Government
Code, 552.010.
comments:
TMB queries the Public Information Database (PI.005)
and enters the open records data monthly into the
Office of Attorney General website. Query results will
be destroyed at the end of the retention period. TMB
does not have records retention access to the data
entered into the OAG website.

series_title:
Sunset Review Report and
Related Documentation
series_description:
Sunset Review Report, agency self-study,
and other correspondence and supporting
documentation related to the Sunset
review process for state agencies.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
ARCHIVES NOTE: Related documentation includes the
agency response to the commission's draft report, and
documentation of the agency's implementation of the
commission's final recommendations. The final version
of the agency self-evaluation report must be
submitted to the Texas State Publications Depository
Program per 13 TAC 3.3(a)(1).
Note 1: Agency will submit records for archival review
when the retention period is met.

series_description:
Master versions of all forms used
internally and externally by the agency,
including subsequent revisions to an
agency form or any associated design or
design modification requests.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
A records retention schedule (i.e., form
SLR 105) that identifies the records that
are created and maintained by an agency,
provides the minimum timeframes the
records must be retained, and includes
instructions for their disposition. This
series may include working files and
documentation of certification and
approval by the Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.
retention_statement: Until Superseded
comments:
Original is retained for 50 years by the State and Local
Records Management Division, Texas State Library and
Archives Commission.

series_description:
Agency copy. Forms used to track the
transmittal of records to/from onsite and
offsite storage areas or a transfer of
physical custody.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
Includes former AIN RR.008.
Agency note: former paper Transmittal Forms (RISN
1.2.006) and Disaster Recovery Services Transmittals
(RISN 1.2.015) no longer created with implementation
of TexLinx software.

series_description:
Logs or similar records listing records
disposed of by an agency, which might
include records series title, dates of
records, and date of disposition.
comments:
CAUTION: Disposition can mean destroyed or
transferred.

series_description:
Agency copy of form RMD 106 granting a
state agency the authorization to store
records at the State Records Center.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable
comments:
RMD 106 is no longer used, but forms for series
currently stored at the Records Center should be
retained.

series_description:
Worksheets or working papers used to
capture records inventory information,
including location tracking and to
document existence and requirements of a
records series.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Records management plans and similar
records that establish policies and
procedures under which records and
information are managed in an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description:
Transmittals and related service
documentation (e.g., form RMD 109) for
disaster recovery services provided by
TSLAC or other entities.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Forms used by TSLAC (e.g., form RMD 113)
or other entities to establish disaster
recovery services, authorize agency staff to
access the media, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Agency note: applies only to the Agency copy of the
RMD-113.

series_description:
Information of significant interest
determined necessary by the Board for
TMB licensees, stakeholders and members
of the public, including disciplinary orders
made against licensed practitioners, board
activities and functions; and pertinent
changes in board rules; disseminated to
each physician practicing in TX, each
health care entity and other board-
designated health care institutions, those
who request in writing, and Texas public
libraries. This series includes the TMB
Bulletin.
retention_statement: Permanent
comments:
CAUTION: Many state publications must be submitted
to the Texas State Publications Depository Program,
TSLAC, by law (Government Code, 441.103-441.105).
The Texas State Library and Archives Commission will
retain a copy of all state publications submitted to it
on a continuing basis, subject to periodic evaluation to
determine if the publication merits further retention.
For additional information and requirements
concerning state publications made available to the
public through the internet, consult the administrative
rules of the Texas State Publications Depository
Program of the Texas State Library and Archives
Commission (13 TAC §§3.1-3.8).

series_description:
Relatively long-lived computer files
containing organized and consistent sets of
complete and accurate electronic records
and associated processing files. Examples
include, but are not limited to, data tables
of relational databases used by
applications or computer programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Vital Record
Note 2: Agency will ensure that all audit requirements
have been fulfilled prior to final disposition.
CAUTION: Records management officers must be
certain before assigning the retention period of AC to
agency master files and application data that the files
do not fall under records series listed elsewhere.

series_description:
Software and hardware access control
system configuration and operations;
configuration and logs from systems and
hardware systems and devices.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Agency-developed automated software
applications, operating system files, and
associated processing files, including job
control language, programs, applications,
scripts, source code, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Vital Record
CAUTION: Software needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the period of time
required to access the records

series_description:
Hardware configurations and associated
processing files.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: Hardware needed for access to electronic
records must be retained for the period of time
required to access the records.

series_description:
Records adequate to specify all technical
characteristics necessary for reading or
processing electronic records; to
document modifications to computer
programs; to access, retrieve, manipulate,
and interpret data in an automated
system; and allow for their timely,
authorized disposition. Records include but
are not limited to: user guides, system or
sub-system definitions, system
specifications, input and output
specifications, system flow charts, program
flow charts, program maintenance logs,
change notices, data element dictionaries,
file layouts, code books or tables, and
other records that explain the meaning,
purpose, structure, logical relationships,
and origin of the data elements.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Note 2: Agency will ensure that all audit requirements
have been fulfilled prior to final disposition.
CAUTION: Hardware and software needed for access
to electronic records must be retained for the period
of time required to access the records.

series_description:
Files needed for electronic data audits.
Records include but are not limited to:
reports showing transactions accepted,
rejected, suspended, and/or processed;
history files or tapes; records of updates to
application files; and security logs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Major features and sections, diagrams and
lists of links, and data sources.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 1 year

series_description: Screen dumps of website content blocks
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_title:
Software Registrations,
Warranties and License
Agreements
series_description:
Records documenting the registration and
licensing of a software application to
activate the software for legal use by the
end users of a state agency, along with
warranties providing that the software will
perform in accordance with functional
specifications.
retention_statement: Life of Asset plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting the request for and
response to help desk tickets received by
divisions or units, such as information
technology.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Biennial information security plan for
protecting the security of the agency’s
information.
retention_statement: Until Superseded

series_description:
Applications, resumes, transcripts, letters
of reference, and similar documents
whose submission by the candidates for
vacant positions is required on the
application form, by application
procedures or in the employment
advertisement.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Notes, memoranda, or reports relating to
the counseling of an employee for work-
related, personal, or substance abuse
problems. Usually maintained at the
supervisorial level except in those agencies
with counseling staff.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years
comments:
Note: Any counseling that involves a medical issue or
a substance abuse problem must be kept confidential.

series_description:
Internal or external announcements or
advertisements of job openings,
promotions, training programs, or
opportunities for overtime.
comments: Filed jointly with HR.002 and HR.007.

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and conditions of
employment of an individual and a
contract with a vendor for temporary
staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
after September 1, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_description:
Includes a contract or agreement
regarding the terms and conditions of
employment of an individual and a
contract with a vendor for temporary
staffing services.
Executed, renewed, or amended on or
before August 31, 2015.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
NOTE: Refer to SB20 (84th Leg.) for retention period
context.

series_description:
Job evaluations, performance appraisals,
or other similar documents used to
evaluate the performance of an employee.

Corrective actions are those actions which
do not affect pay, status, or tenure and are
imposed to correct or improve an
employee's job performance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years
comments:
CAUTION: If during the retention period of this records
series, any part of this series is used to document or
support a disciplinary action under 3.1.021, all
documentation used to support the disciplinary action
must be retained for the minimum period under
3.1.021.

series_description:
Disciplinary actions are those actions
which may affect pay, status, or tenure.
They are imposed to discipline an
employee whose conduct is harmful to the
best interests of the state, the agency, or
the employee work force; and for failure
to improve performance or conduct
following imposition of corrective action.
May include cause for demotion,
suspension, or dismissal, and the reasons
for failure to give written notice of
resignation. May also be used to document
evidence of employee self-improvement
efforts, as well as favorable and
unfavorable communication.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms or similar records used to create or
change information concerning the
records of an employee including pay
grade, position classification, evaluation
date, and termination of employment.

Job descriptions, including all associated
task or skill statements, for positions in an
agency.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Vital Record
This series is the compilation of all job descriptions by
position not by employee.

series_description:
The job description signed by the
employee and filed in the employee's
personnel folder.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description:
Criminal history record information on job
applicants or agency employees obtained
from the Department of Public Safety
(DPS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
CAUTION: An agency that is authorized to obtain a
criminal history record information from DPS must
refer to its agency's legislation or see Subchapter F,
Chapter 411, Government Code for appropriate
retention and use of this information.

series_description:
Certificates of completion, certifications,
licenses, transcripts, test scores, or similar
records documenting the training, testing,
certification, licensing, or continuing
education achievements of an employee.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description: Federal reporting form (Form I-9).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_description: Awards, incentives, tenure, etc.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Agreements between employee and
agency authorizing certain actions,
including acknowledgement forms,
telecommuting agreements,
outside/secondary employment
authorizations, or other documentation
that show proof of receipt and awareness
of and adherence to agency policies and
procedures.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Employee or applicant requests for
reasonable accommodation under the ADA
(Americans with Disabilities Act).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years

series_description:
Documentation used to start, modify, or
stop all voluntary or required deductions
from payroll, including garnishment or
other court ordered attachments.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes 1099, W2, FICA and other tax
records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Used to make increases or decreases to
employees' gross pay, FICA, retirement, or
in the computation of taxes.

Employer’s copy of “Employees’
Withholding Exemption Certificate”.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years
comments:
Vital Record
Starting in July 2017 employees began making W-4
selections in CAPPS.
Agency only retains forms of new employees and
authorizations from employees, when entries must be
made on an employee's behalf. All other selections
are made by employees through the CAPPS website
and the TMB has no records retention access to that
data.
Any forms received by the agency are considered to be
obsolete after one year from receipt or upon
separation of the employee, which ever comes first.

series_description:
Records and documentation relating to
unemployment compensation claims,
including reimbursement of funds
disbursed by Texas Workforce Commission
(TWC).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Forms used to deposit employee’s
earnings into a specified personal account.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
Vital Record
Starting in July 2017 employees began entering direct
deposit changes in CAPPS.
Agency only retains forms of new employees and
authorizations from employees, when entries must be
made on an employee's behalf. All other changes are
made by employees through the CAPPS website and
the TMB has no records retention access to that data.
Any forms received by the agency are considered to be
superseded at the end of the fiscal year + one year (or
upon separation of the employee, which ever comes
first.)

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for regular
employees and apprenticeship programs.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 5 years

series_description:
Affirmative action plans for contractors
and subcontractors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_description:
Records relating to review and monitoring
of job classifications within an agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Work, duty, shift, crew or case schedules,
rosters or assignments
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Reports to the agency from TWC or its
predecessor pertaining to employees.

Any internally distributed manuals,
guidelines or similar records that define
agency wide policies and procedures
concerning the personnel of the agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:
Any reports compiled by an agency on
aspects of personnel staffing, including
listing of all staff by program or name,
staff hired during a month, detailed listings
of employees within its organizational
structure, position vacancies, analysis of
turnover rates and seasonality of
employment, etc.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 5 years
comments:
Reports are considered superseded each year on 8/31
when the fiscal year report is generated.

series_description:
Aptitude, competency, or skills tests and
checklists required of job applicants or of
current personnel to qualify for promotion
or transfer, including validation records.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
This series is the blank test, not the test completed by
the applicant.
CAUTION: One copy of each different master test
(different in terms of either questions or
administration procedures) should be retained for the
period indicated.

series_description:
Includes documentation used to complete
EEO (Equal Employment Opportunity)
reports.

Reports, studies, aggregated or summary
data, and similar documentation compiled
to monitor and demonstrate compliance
with the federal Equal Pay Act.

All records supporting transactions made
by Finance staff into CAPPS affecting an
employees pay or leave.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Used to create employee leave balances,
to transfer leave balances when an
employee transfers positions, to adjust
carry-over balances at end of fiscal year, to
correct errors on leave accumulation, and
to close out leave accounts on separated
employees.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Records documenting individual
employee’s hours worked, including work
schedules and documentation evidencing
adherence to or deviation from normal
hours for those employees working on
fixed schedules.
comments:
Starting in July 2017, timesheets were completed
electronically through the CAPPS website. TMB has no
records retention access to the data.

series_title:
Time Off and/or Sick Leave
Requests
series_description:
Requests and authorizations for vacation,
compensatory, sick, Family and Medical
Leave Act (FMLA), sick pool leave, and
other types of authorized leave, and
supporting documentation.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years
comments:
Includes all non-verbal requests for time off in
advance, regardless of the type of leave used.

series_description:
Records documenting number of hours
transferred in and out of comprehensive
sick leave pool.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Claims; invoices; statements; copies of
checks and purchase orders; expenditure
authorizations; ledgers; encumbrance,
purchase, general journal, expenditure,
and special vouchers; and similar records
that serve to document disbursements,
including those documenting claims for
and reimbursement to employees for
travel and other expenses.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Bill copies or stubs, statements, billing
registers, account cards, deposit warrants,
cash receipts, credit card receipts, receipts
books, cash transfers, daily cash reports,
reconciliations, general journal vouchers,
special vouchers, and similar records that
serve to document money owed to or
received by a state agency and its
collection or receipt.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 3 years

series_description:
Periodic reports compiled from
information entered into the Uniform
Statewide Accounting System (USAS).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments: USAS is the system of record.

series_description:
Records documenting the internal and
external procedural requirements with
respect to the accounting department of a
state agency.
retention_statement: Until Superseded plus 3 years

series_description:

"pristine investigation file" when the case receives a
litigation case number.
Note 4: Records are confidential as required by one of
the following sections of the Medical Practice Act,
Occupations Code §§ 153.006, 154.053. 155.00,
155.008, 155.055, 155.058, 159.003, 160.005,
160.006, and/or 160.007.

series_title:
Investigation Files - Closed -
Non-Jurisdictional
(On or after 1/1/19)
series_description:
Case closed with NJ resolution. Retain
entire file during retention period.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 3 years

series_title:
Investigation Files - Closed -
Jurisdictional Not Filed
(On or after 1/1/19)
series_description:
Case closed with JNF resolution. Retain
entire file during retention period.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years

series_title:
Investigation Files - Closed -
Dismissed After
Investigation
(On or after 1/1/19)
series_description:
Case closed with DI resolution. Retain
entire file during retention period.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 10 years

series_description: Inspection Form
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 7 years
comments:
OBA inspections are on a 4 year cycle. Five cycles of
OBA forms are available.

series_description:
This series begins when a copy of the
"pristine investigation file" is assigned a
litigation case number and is modified as
needed through the litigation process.
Also, includes remedial plans offered
during the investigative process.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)

series_title:
Attorney Notes, Drafts and
Working Papers
series_description:
Materials prepared by or for an attorney in
the course of business that include
handwritten notes, draft documents,
research, and working papers that are not
essential to the fulfillment of statutory
obligations or to document agency
functions.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Only the key retention documents from
the Litigation Files - Active (EN.201) are
moved to this series and all remaining
documents are destroyed under EN.201.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years

series_description:
Signed originals of remedial plans and
board orders.
retention_statement: Permanent

series_description:
Records that support the required
reporting to the NPDB.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Federation of State Medical
Board Reports
series_description:
Documents used to transmit and support
reporting of board actions to the FSMB.
retention_statement: Fiscal Year End plus 1 year
comments:
Original documents that are part of another series
remain in and are retained for the period specified by
that series.

series_title:
Federation of State Medical
Board Reporting
Verification
series_description:
Documents received from FSMB, and
documents identifying, supporting, and
correcting any reporting errors.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Original documents that are part of another series
remain in and are retained for the period specified by
that series.

series_description:
Documents used to monitor respondents
compliance with remedial plans or board
orders and, when appropriate, to
document non-compliance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year

series_title:
Expert Panel Member and
Billing Consultant
Information
series_description:
Includes contracts, questionnaire and
personal election form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_description:
Includes contracts and personal election
form.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 4 years

series_title:
Physician Professional
Liability Suits and Claims
Files
series_description:
Professional liability suits or claims filed
against physicians.
retention_statement: As Long as Administratively Valuable

series_description:
Queries of the Enforcement History
(EN.101) database that are used to
support other records series. Query
results will be destroyed at the end of the
retention period, but can be recreated at
any time.

This series begins with a complaint
received about an individual or entity that
is or should be regulated by the agency
and continues until closed with a valid
disposition code.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled)
comments:
If closed RH, files became EN.002; all other dispositions
became EN.003.
Note 4: Records are confidential as required by one of
the following sections of the Medical Practice Act,
Occupations Code §§ 153.006, 154.053. 155.00,
155.008, 155.055, 155.058, 159.003, 160.005,
160.006, and/or 160.007.

series_title:
Investigation Files - Closed
as RH or Remedial Plan
(Closed before 1/1/19)
series_description:
Closed - RH (Prior to 2006, included all
dispositions).
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years
comments:
Note 4: Records are confidential as required by one of
the following sections of the Medical Practice Act,
Occupations Code §§ 153.006, 154.053. 155.00,
155.008, 155.055, 155.058, 159.003, 160.005,
160.006, and/or 160.007.
106 No. #90-503-001
Refer to EN.201 for resolution types.

series_description: Case closed with NJ resolution.

This series begins when a copy of the

needed through the litigation process.

series_description:
Combines all litigation files closed by any
resolution (remedial plan, dismissed at
hearings, agreed orders, orders resulting
from SOAH or court cases) before 1/1/19.
Also, includes remedial plans offered
during the investigative process.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 20 years

series_description:
Documents used to monitor respondents
compliance with remedial plans or board
orders and, when appropriate, to
document non-compliance.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 1 year
comments:
Prior to 2006, compliance files were interfiled with
former AIN EN.005 (now AIN EN.011) and will be
destroyed when the retention period for EN.011 is
met. 106 No. 96-503-015.
Note 4: Records are confidential as required by one of
the following sections of the Medical Practice Act,
Occupations Code §§ 153.006, 154.053. 155.00,
155.008, 155.055, 155.058, 159.003, 160.005,

series_description:
Complaints and other unsolicited feedback
received by an agency from the public
concerning the agency and records
pertaining to the resolution of complaints.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments:
CAUTION: If a complaint becomes the subject of
litigation, it must be included in and is subject to the
minimum retention period of item number 1.1.048.

series_title:
Meetings, Agendas and
Minutes or Audiovisual
Recordings of Closed
series_description:
Agendas, minutes, or audiovisual
recordings of closed meetings of state
boards, commissions, committees, and
councils.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 2 years
comments: SEE caution comment at item number 1.1.058.

series_description:
Program referrals and all related forms,
correspondence, reports, agreements,
documents, and disposition.
retention_statement: After Closed (or terminated, completed, expired, or settled) plus 25 years
comments:
Date of Referral - Date PHP is notified of a person who
may need to be evaluated or to participate in the
program.
Documents are confidential by law (Ch. 167, Medical
Practice Act).
